{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Shimobaba Kofun | native_name =馬場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimobaba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|18|50|N|130|37|22|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (occasionally) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|50|N|130|37|22|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shimobaba Kofun'''|下馬場古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kusano neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle [[alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving [[grave goods]]; however, but a cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from [[Chikugo-Kusano Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]]. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimobaba Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shimobaba Kofun | native_name =馬場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimobaba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|18|50|N|130|37|22|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (occasionally) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|50|N|130|37|22|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shimobaba Kofun'''|下馬場古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kusano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]; however, but a cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Kusano Station|Chikugo-Kusano Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]]. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14859 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Kusano Station|Chikugo-Kusano Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]]. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimobaba Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shimobaba Kofun | native_name =馬場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimobaba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|18|50|N|130|37|22|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (occasionally) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|50|N|130|37|22|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shimobaba Kofun'''|下馬場古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kusano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]; however, but a cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Kusano Station|Chikugo-Kusano Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]]. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14859 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]; however, but a cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Kusano Station|Chikugo-Kusano Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]]. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14859 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]; however, but a cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Kusano Station|Chikugo-Kusano Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]]. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14859 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shimobaba Kofun'''|下馬場古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kusano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]; however, but a cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Kusano Station|Chikugo-Kusano Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyūdai Main Line|Kyūdai Main Line]]. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14859 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shimobaba Kofun'''|下馬場古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kusano neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan}} at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}; however, but a cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chikugo-Kusano Station|en|Chikugo-Kusano Station|WD=Q4778776|lt=Chikugo-Kusano Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|WD=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line}}. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Shimobaba Kofun'''|下馬場古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kusano neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191942|title=下馬場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the Yakushi Shimokita Kofun and the Yakushi Shimominami Kofun, which no longer exist, it was part of the Yoshiki Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Shimobaba Kofun is located on a gentle {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains, which limits the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters and a height of approximately fve meters, with a horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the west. It has a length of 12 meters and is divided into a corridor and a front and rear chamber. It is thought to have been constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. The tomb has been robbed in antiquity, and there are no surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}; however, but a cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' was found at the base of the Kofun, and a female figurine clay figurine with a sash was discovered in the outer area during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2012. The excavation also confirmed that the tumulus was originally 42 meters in diameter and had been surrounded by a five-meter wide moat. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]], and decorations are painted on almost the entire wall of the burial chamber, although they are faded and unclear. The decorations are mainly red-colored concentric circles, with continuous triangular patterns and ship-like shapes. It appears that some blue colors were also used. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Chikugo-Kusano Station|en|Chikugo-Kusano Station|WD=Q4778776|lt=Chikugo-Kusano Station|1=Chikugo-Kusano Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|WD=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|1=Kyūdai Main Line}}. It was formerly open to the public, but is now only open on a couple of days a year for preservation.
